name: Michał Ratajczak
title: CEO & Co-Founder
headline: Founder & CEO @ Gridaly | Forbes 30u30 | Sigma² | Event&Networking Expert
about:
As the CEO and Co-Founder of Gridaly, an all-in-one B2B event management software, I lead the business strategy, sales, marketing, and company management. 

Gridaly's mission is to boost the event experience. Gridaly's vision is to ensure that all event processes are seamless. Whether the purpose is entertainment, business, networking, or any other, events should serve their goals without causing any distractions. We want attendees to fully immerse in the event experience without being overwhelmed by organizational matters. 

I have a Master's degree in Corporate Finance Management from SGH Warsaw School of Economics and a Bachelor's degree in Automotive Engineering from Warsaw University of Technology (with annual exchange at Politecnico di Torino). I combine my engineering and technology knowledge with my business development and management experience to create innovative solutions for the event industry.

I am passionate about promoting startups and innovations, and I initiated GridalyLab, an educational project that helps people navigate digital transformation and remote work. I also write a newsletter about the latest trends and best practices in event and marketing technology.

*Conference BIO / LONG *
Michal Ratajczak / CEO @ Gridaly 
"CEO and co-founder of Gridaly, a startup offering comprehensive B2B event management software.

Ratajczak is dedicated to promoting the power of events in accomplishing diverse objectives, including networking, business goals, marketing and personal development.

He frequently addresses audiences at European technology conferences, sharing expertise on topics such as effective networking, new technologies, and startups."

*Conference BIO / SHORT *
Promoting the power of events for diverse goals: networking, business, and personal growth.

name: Aleks Pajewski
title: Co-Founder & Chief Product Officer
headline: Co-Founder & CPO @ VitVio | Forbes 30u30 | Sigma²
about:
As a Co-Founder and CPO at VitVio, my mission is to deploy products to enhance the safety and efficiency of hospitals. We achieve this by utilizing computer vision and ambient sensing to digitize operating rooms in real-time 3D. This provides clear insights into who is performing which tasks, when, and why during surgery. Our system then automates routine tasks, such as logging surgical phases and coordinating processes. The ultimate goal is to improve the experience for both patients and staff, as well as boost hospital ROI by increasing OR throughput and reducing the administrative load on medical teams.

Before VitVio, I was as a Product Manager at AiFi, a global leader in AI-powered store automation technology. In this role, I led the strategy and development for three product groups across two teams: Customer & Retailer Apps, Core Infrastructure Services, and Data Annotation Service for AI training. My contributions helped the company grow from just one store to over 120 worldwide over 3 years.

name: Marcin Waryszak
title: Chief Executive Officer & Founder
headline: Children’s Mental Health | Forbes 30 under 30 | 🔴 TEDx Speaker | Sigma² Fellow | Founder
about:
My first ’business’? I was 12 years old. Together with a group of friends (co-founders!), we invested in a kettle, tea, instant coffee, coconut shrimp, chocolate, and a train ticket to Berlin. On our first day there, we made a Bounty cake and started selling. The investment paid off, we spent all the money having fun and then returned to Warsaw, back to school.

Now I am an entrepreneur and strategist dedicated to global impact-driven ventures, armed with a solid academic foundation in mathematics and economics.

My expertise extends to advisory roles for the government sector, where I have contributed to legal processes and served as a constant advisor or one-off consultant to key entities like the Polish President, Ministry of Education, Ministry of Health, and Council of Ministers' Social Committee. I am also an expert at i4KIDS Working Group - a selected set of Children's Hospitals Decision Makers, Public Administration, and Physicians dedicated to introducing paediatric innovations into healthcare systems globally.

My secret sauce - building partnerships and connecting the dots - I thrive on helping individuals and institutions reach their full potential. My journey includes serving on the management board for third-sector organizations, being a guest academic teacher (SGH Warsaw School of Economics, University of Wroclaw), or founding two impact-driven startups that have become local market leaders and are on the path to worldwide success (Calmsie, Envirly - VC-backed).

As a seasoned speaker, panelist and mentor, I actively participate in international economic and business congresses, contributing to the global dialogue on impactful entrepreneurship and effective altruism. 

____________________

🔹 Forbes 30 under 30
🔹 Forbes 25 under 25
🔹 '50 most creative people in business' Award
🔹 1 of 10 most inspiring young entrepreneurs by Forbes
🔹 'Entrepreneur of the Year' - University of Warsaw

name: Mateusz Kowalczyk
title: CEO & Co-Founder
headline: CEO @ Foodsi
about:
As the CEO and Co-Founder of Foodsi, I lead a global marketplace that helps retailers sell surplus food, reducing waste and supporting sustainability. With over 5 years of experience in this role, I have successfully scaled the business and expanded its reach to various markets. I am also a mentor at foodtech.ac, where I support startups and innovators in the food technology sector, sharing my expertise in business strategy, organization building, and fundraising. 
My mission is to create a more sustainable food system and empower entrepreneurs to make a positive impact.

name: Mateusz Kussowski
title: Huawei
headline: Software Engineer @ Huawei | CS Student @ University of Warsaw
about:
My passion for algorithm design began with competitive programming in high school. This drive led me to become a multi-year Laureate and Finalist in the Polish National Olympiad in Informatics and a Finalist in the Polish Olympiad in Artificial Intelligence.

I'm currently building on this foundation as a Computer Science student at the University of Warsaw, where I maintain a 4.85 GPA (Top 5%).

Alongside my studies, I work as a Part-time Software Engineer on Huawei's AI Team. I was invited to this role after a successful internship where I was named an 'Excellent Intern' for my work. Today, I develop and evaluate advanced NLP systems, focusing on model performance and content quality.

I also stay deeply connected to the competitive programming community and remain engaged with the Polish and Baltic Olympiad in Informatics, including designing and testing new algorithmic challenges.

Outside of work and study, I'm always building side projects, which you can find on my GitHub. I'm very open to collaborating on interesting new ideas. I'm always looking to connect and discuss new challenges in AI, optimization problems, and complex algorithm design.

name: Matt Kozak
title: Chief Technology Officer
headline: CTO @ Pstryk // I build things. // climate tech // sustainability // AI
about:
Changing the Polish energy market as a CTO at Pstryk - the most advanced dynamic tariff provider in the country!

With almost two decades of hands-on experience in software development, systems architecture, networking, and engineering best practices, I build AI-based solutions using either cloud-based or locally run large language model (LLM) models.
If you want to discuss LoRaWAN and smart cities, I'm more than happy to chat!

I am passionate about green tech, climate change, sustainability, and livable cities. 
Because of that, I've founded Work in Green, a platform that helps people find their next job at green tech companies. 
I am also a member of the Greentech Alliance, a community that supports and connects green tech startups.
I am always eager to learn new skills, adopt new technologies, and collaborate with other professionals who share my vision and values.

name: Oskar Wójcikiewicz
title: Co-Founder & CEO
headline: Co-Founder @ slimtwin. Weight loss is broken, and we’re fixing it.
about:
👨‍💻 I started writing code when I was 13, making mods for my favorite game. That turned into something bigger — I built a software agency in that niche, and we quickly became one of the top developers in the space, working with some of the biggest gaming communities in the US, UK, Canada, and Australia, serving 1.5 million unique players.

⚙️ I love working on backend systems. My main stack is TypeScript, and I build with Node.js (Express, NestJS) while developing REST, WebSockets, gRPC, and tRPC APIs. I’ve worked with relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and NoSQL (MongoDB, Redis) databases, using message queues (RabbitMQ, BullMQ, SQS) for distributed workloads. I’m also big on containerization and Docker, deeply interested in distributed systems, and have hands-on experience setting up CI/CD workflows from scratch.

🫀 Beyond software engineering, I’m deeply passionate about longevity, shaped by both personal experience — losing 85 kg in 12 months — and a strong belief in technology’s role in transforming healthcare. I see a future where, by democratizing access, dying from preventable diseases becomes a choice for everyone.

🎓 As a Fellow in the MD Fellowship program — a prestigious mentoring initiative for ambitious individuals aged 16-22 — I have the opportunity to learn from my mentor and refine my skills as both an entrepreneur and engineer.

🚀 Always happy to connect! If you're working on something interesting — whether in software, longevity, healthcare, or anything that sparks curiosity — let’s talk!

name: Piotr Grzegorczyk
title: Co-Founder & CEO
headline: CEO at Deepflare.ai | Better vaccines faster
about:
I am theoretical mathematician turned biotech entrepreneur. What started as a side project for modelling COVID-19 mutations, turned into a lifetime project of supporting development of better vaccines, faster.

Deepflare's AI platform allows vaccine developers to move beyond binding affinity and predict true T-cell immunogenicity. We have de-risked and accelerated vaccine discovery process for pharma and biotech companies around the world, including OSIVAX, Ceva, and Progeneer.

I am proud to have been supported on my entrepreneurial journey by organisations such as EWOR, Sigma Squared, and On Deck, and pay it forward through mentoring and angel investing in early-stage AI-first projects.

I split my time before Warsaw, Poland and Cambridge, MA.

name: Snizhana S.
title: Chief Operating Officer
headline: Chief Operating Officer @ Techosystem | Operations Management · Strategic Planning · P&L · AI-Native Operator | I build the operating system so founders can build the product.
about: I build operating systems. Founders bring the vision — I make it executable.  COO with 15+ years across financial management, operations, and organisational scaling in tech environments. Currently leading operations at Techosystem, a paid union of 100+ Ukrainian tech companies and investors.  Core areas of expertise: → Operations Management & Process Improvement — designing the infrastructure that lets teams scale without chaos → P&L Ownership & Financial Management — $75M+ in project finance, budget control, investor relations → Strategic Planning & Organisational Leadership — OKRs, governance, decision rights, cross-functional team management → Business Development & Stakeholder Relations — EU institutions, VC networks, government bodies, international partners → AI-Augmented Operations — I ship working tools, not decks about tools  On the last point: I independently built and deployed a full production intelligence platform — no engineering team — using a modern no-code/low-code stack. It runs in production. That is what AI-native operations looks like in practice.  Background: Before moving into COO roles, I spent 10+ years in project finance, structuring and closing transactions with a combined value of $75M+ across digital infrastructure and tech. That financial rigour shapes how I run operations today.
